src/flit/foundation/key

      • `$`(k: Key): string

      • `==`(a, b: Key): bool

        newGlobalKey
      • newGlobalKey(label: string = ""): GlobalKey
        newObjectKey
      • newObjectKey(obj: pointer): Key

      • newValueKey(value: string): Key
      • newValueKey[T](value: T): ValueKey[T]

Keys identify widgets across rebuilds so the framework can preserve element (and state) identity when the widget tree is regenerated. Mirrors Flutter's Key hierarchy.

When two sibling widgets have the same runtime type, the framework decides whether to reuse the existing element for either of them by comparing keys. Without a key, sibling reorders force a re-mount; with a key, the element follows the keyed widget to its new slot and any State it owns is preserved.

Types

GlobalKey = ref object of Key
  currentElement*: pointer
A Key whose identity persists across the entire app and that the framework can resolve to the currently mounted Element. Equality is by reference (two distinct GlobalKey instances never compare equal). 
Key = ref object of RootObj
  case kind*: KeyKind
  of kkUnique:
    uniqueId*: int
  of kkValue:
    value*: string
  of kkGlobal:
    label*: string
  of kkObject:
    objRef*: pointer
A reconciliation key. Variant fields are selected by kind. 
KeyKind = enum
  kkUnique, kkValue, kkGlobal, kkObject
Discriminator for the Key variant types.
  • kkUnique: per-allocation unique counter (newUniqueKey).
  • kkValue: equality by a wrapped string / value (newValueKey).
  • kkGlobal: equality by reference identity, addressable from anywhere in the tree (newGlobalKey).
  • kkObject: equality by an external object pointer (newObjectKey).
ValueKey[T] = ref object of Key
  typedValue*: T
A Key that wraps an arbitrary typed value T. The string form is what's used for equality and hashing (so T needs $T).

Procs

proc `$`(k: Key): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Debug representation. Used by debugDescribe to show keys in inspector output. 
proc `==`(a, b: Key): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Equality across the variant types. Two nil keys are equal. Different kinds never compare equal. 
proc hash(k: Key): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Hashes a key. nil hashes to 0. The hash incorporates the kind and the value-determining field for that kind. 
proc newGlobalKey(label: string = ""): GlobalKey {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns a GlobalKey whose label is just a debug aid. Each call returns a distinct key. 
proc newObjectKey(obj: pointer): Key {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns a Key whose equality is by the wrapped pointer. Useful for tying a widget's identity to an external Nim object. 
proc newUniqueKey(): Key {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns a brand-new Key that doesn't equal any other key. Useful when you want to force-recreate a subtree on every rebuild. 
proc newValueKey(value: string): Key {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns a Key whose equality is the string value. Two ValueKey("a") instances are equal. 
proc newValueKey[T](value: T): ValueKey[T]
Generic variant that remembers the original typed value as well as its string form.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 04:58:21 UTC
